spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the headline Overscroll effect on non-root scrollers surface (non-root, overscroll-behavior); residual gaps in spec vocabulary: no obvious gaps.

interactive.rationale: The previous generic probe controls were removed because they did not test this feature. Re-review the remaining concept in chrome-devtools-mcp and add feature-specific interaction if the page is now only static content.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5195768556290048 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Re-review after generic probe removal
openQuestions.detail: The unrelated generated live-probe controls were removed on 2026-06-04. Use chrome-devtools-mcp to re-test the remaining concept UI; if the page is now static or lacks a feature-specific observable behavior, add a real control, readout, server-backed route, or exact feature probe rather than restoring the generic panel.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on overscroll-behavior-comparison
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: Solid concept page for Overscroll effect on non-root scrollers: interactive, design-system clean, and references the chromestatus entry. Tighten edge-case coverage and the spec-vocabulary surface area to take it from good to canonical. 2026-06-04 maintenance removed the unrelated generic live-probe panel; this critique needs chrome-devtools-mcp re-review before treating the interactivity score as current.
